name: Dolphin 2.5 Mixtral 8x7B
archDisplay: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (8 experts, top-2 routing; Mixtral 8x7B base)
note: QLoRA fine-tune of Mixtral 8x7B (Apache-2.0) with refusals filtered out and Dolphin-Coder/MagiCoder data added; a benchmark of the late-2023 'uncensored' MoE fine-tune wave.
tpsNote: Q4 (~26GB) slightly exceeds a single RTX 4090's 24GB VRAM; typically run with partial CPU offload or reduced context.
verificationNotes:
Confirmed real: HF repo dphn/dolphin-2.5-mixtral-8x7b exists, fine-tune of Mixtral-8x7B by Eric Hartford / Cognitive Computations, Apache-2.0, base 32K context fine-tuned at 16K, trained with Dolphin-Coder/MagiCoder data and refusals filtered (confirmed on model card and erichartford.com blog). releaseDate corrected/confirmed precisely via HF API createdAt field = 2023-12-14T00:45:47Z, matching the record exactly (training itself ran Dec 9-11, 2023, blog post published Dec 22, 2023). No results rows were present to verify. No changes needed. ROUND-7 EXHAUSTIVE-SEARCH RECORD (2026-07-15, methodology-diversified per native-language/regional-leaderboard instructions): No real, citable benchmark results found for Dolphin 2.5 Mixtral 8x7B (dphn/dolphin-2.5-mixtral-8x7b) after a widened search. This is not a Chinese or Japanese vendor (Cognitive Computations / Eric Hartford is an English-language, US-based open-source fine-tuner), so I focused on angle 3 of the brief: Artificial Analysis, LMSYS/Arena, OpenRouter, and Wayback/vendor-page checks.

Checked and found nothing:
- HF model card (huggingface.co/dphn/dolphin-2.5-mixtral-8x7b) — no eval numbers, confirmed by direct fetch.
- TheBloke/dolphin-2.5-mixtral-8x7b-GGUF quantized model card — no eval numbers, confirmed by direct fetch.
- HuggingFace Open LLM Leaderboard discussion #517 — Dolphin Mixtral models repeatedly FAILED evaluation (weights not in safetensors format at the time), so no scores were ever produced for this model on that leaderboard.
- OpenRouter — the model page for ehartford/dolphin-2.5-mixtral-8x7b returns not-found/no listing (only the later Dolphin 2.6 Mixtral variant is listed under a different org path, cognitivecomputations/dolphin-mixtral-8x7b, which is a different model).
- Artificial Analysis — no dedicated benchmark page for this Dolphin fine-tune; only covers base Mixtral 8x7B Instruct, a distinct model.
- Eric Hartford's own blog post (erichartford.com/dolphin-25-mixtral-8x7b) — training/release details only, no benchmark numbers.
- web.archive.org — direct fetch tool blocked access to archive.org, so Wayback snapshots of the vendor page could not be checked this round.

One dead end explicitly ruled out: telnyx.com/llm-library page and a couple of similar SEO aggregator sites (anakin.ai) claim specific numbers (Arena Elo "1063", MMLU "~70.8%"). On direct fetch and inspection these are confirmed auto-generated/template comparison pages with NO source citation ("community benchmarks" unattributed), internally inconsistent (the same Elo number "1063" is separately attributed in the same text to both Dolphin 2.5 and to Llama 2 Chat 13B), and there is no evidence dolphin-2.5-mixtral-8x7b was ever actually rated in LMSYS Chatbot Arena (it's a niche community fine-tune, not a model LMSYS evaluated). These numbers are not trustworthy and were deliberately NOT reported. A separate anakin.ai page cites an informal Reddit quiz score ("15/18", "13/18") which is not a standard benchmark and has no taxonomy fit.

Conclusion: this appears to be a genuine case of a model whose creator never published standard benchmark results, and whose obsolete quant format prevented it from ever completing Open LLM Leaderboard evaluation. No additions recommended. Suggest updating verificationNotes on the corpus file to record this final, widened-search null result so future rounds don't re-attempt the same dead ends (HF card, TheBloke GGUF card, Open LLM Leaderboard #517, OpenRouter, Artificial Analysis, erichartford.com blog, and the telnyx/anakin.ai low-quality aggregator numbers).
